title: Install Counter-Balance Weights and Restart the Operator Station
summary: Install the counter-balance weights using four M10 socket head screws with lock washers, tighten to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs), account for acceptable carriage drift behavior if the gripper is not mounted, reinstall guarding if necessary, and then restart the operator station using the separately referenced startup procedure.
when_to_use: Use when reinstalling the OptiSweep counter-balance weights as documented in section 7.3.3.2 INSTALLATION, including restoring guarding if needed and returning the operator station to service by following the referenced startup procedure on page 66.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete operator-station startup procedure; the restart steps are not provided in this source section and are only referenced to page 66.
Do not use this runbook for general low-risk support handling; the source context includes a 20 kg (44 lb) weight assembly, guarding access, and possible carriage movement.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the mechanical installation of the counter-balance weights, tighten the hardware to the specified torque, restore guarding if necessary, and initiate restart using the separately referenced startup procedure.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe for general support use.
The counter-balance weights are about 20 kg (44 lb) total.
Once the weights are in place, if the gripper is not yet mounted, the carriage may begin to move.
The documented carriage movement is acceptable because the weights will drift to the lower hard stop.
The procedure includes guarding reinstallation and moving mechanical components.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the counter-balance weight mounting area
Tooling to install and tighten M10 socket head screws
Torque tool capable of approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs)
Access to top and/or bottom side guarding
Access to the operator station restart procedure on page 66

access_required: Access to the counter-balance weight mounting area
Access to top and/or bottom side guarding
Access to the operator station restart procedure on page 66
system_preconditions: Counter-balance weights are ready to be installed.
Guarding can be reinstalled if it was previously removed.
If the gripper is not yet mounted, personnel must be prepared for acceptable carriage drift to the lower hard stop after weight installation.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 7.3.3.2 INSTALLATION, page 112

visual_references.description: Figure 7-4 Counterbalance Weight; use as the available packet artifact showing the counterbalance weight assembly and related hardware context.

steps.title: Install the counter-balance weights with specified hardware
steps.purpose: Secure the counter-balance weights onto the assembly using the documented fasteners.
steps.instruction: Hold the counter-balance weights in place and install four (4) M10 socket head screws with the lock washer. Account for the documented total weight of about 20 kg (44 lb) during handling.
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance weights are held in position and secured with four M10 socket head screws and lock washers.
steps.healthy_condition: All four specified screws and lock washers are installed and the weights are held in place.
steps.failure_condition: The weights cannot be held securely, required hardware is missing, or the weights are not fully installed.

actions.label: Position counter-balance weights
actions.exact_action: Hold the counter-balance weights in place.

actions.label: Install mounting hardware
actions.exact_action: Install four (4) M10 socket head screws with the lock washer.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance weight assembly and the hardware context associated with the counter-balance weights.

stop_or_escalate_if: The 20 kg (44 lb) weight assembly cannot be safely held or positioned.
Required M10 socket head screws or lock washers are unavailable.
The weights cannot be secured in place.

steps.title: Tighten the counter-balance mounting screws
steps.purpose: Apply the documented installation torque to the installed hardware.
steps.instruction: Tighten the installed screws to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).
steps.expected_result: The installed counter-balance mounting screws are tightened to the documented torque value.
steps.healthy_condition: All installed screws are tightened to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).
steps.failure_condition: The screws are not tightened to the specified torque or cannot be torqued as required.

actions.label: Torque mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Tighten the installed screws to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).

stop_or_escalate_if: A torque tool capable of approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs) is not available.
The installed screws cannot be tightened to the documented torque.

steps.title: Verify acceptable carriage movement condition
steps.purpose: Recognize and correctly interpret the documented carriage movement behavior after the weights are installed.
steps.instruction: Note that once the weights are in place, if the gripper is not yet mounted, the carriage may begin to move. The documented movement is acceptable because the weights will drift to the lower hard stop.
steps.expected_result: The technician recognizes that drift to the lower hard stop is an acceptable documented condition when the gripper is not mounted.
steps.healthy_condition: If movement occurs under the stated condition, it is limited to the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
steps.failure_condition: Movement occurs in a way not described by the source or creates an unsafe or uncontrolled condition.

actions.label: Observe carriage behavior
actions.exact_action: Note that once the weights are in place, if the gripper is not yet mounted, the carriage may begin to move.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance assembly context related to the documented drift behavior after installation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Carriage movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.

steps.title: Reinstall guarding if necessary
steps.purpose: Restore machine guarding after the counter-balance installation work.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side guarding, if necessary.
steps.expected_result: Any required top and/or bottom side guarding is reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Guarding that was removed for access is restored as needed.
steps.failure_condition: Required guarding is not reinstalled when needed.

actions.label: Reinstall guarding
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side guarding, if necessary.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance area context; use the available figure to orient to the assembly while restoring guarding.

stop_or_escalate_if: Guarding access or fitment does not allow restoration to the documented condition.

steps.title: Restart the operator station using the referenced procedure
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the separately documented startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station restart is initiated using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The restart is performed by following the separate page 66 procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted or the referenced startup procedure is unavailable.

actions.label: Use referenced startup procedure
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66 is not available.
The operator station does not restart when following the referenced procedure.

success_criteria: The counter-balance weights are installed with four M10 socket head screws and lock washers.
The installed screws are tightened to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).
Any required top and/or bottom side guarding is reinstalled.
The operator station is restarted using the referenced page 66 procedure.
healthy_conditions: Counter-balance weights are secured in place with the specified hardware.
Torque is applied to the documented value of approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).
If the gripper is not mounted, any carriage movement matches the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
The operator station restart is handed off to the referenced startup procedure.
failure_conditions: The 20 kg (44 lb) weight assembly cannot be safely handled or positioned.
Required hardware is missing or cannot be installed.
The screws cannot be tightened to approximately 51 Nm (65 ft-lbs).
Carriage movement occurs in a way not described as acceptable by the source.
The referenced startup procedure on page 66 is unavailable or restart cannot be completed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the weight assembly cannot be safely handled due to the documented 20 kg (44 lb) load.
Escalate if carriage movement appears unsafe or differs from the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
Escalate if guarding cannot be restored.
Escalate if the separate operator station startup procedure on page 66 is required but unavailable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance weight assembly and related hardware context for installation orientation.

missing_details: This source section does not provide the detailed restart steps for "Starting The Operator Station"; only the page 66 reference is available.
The packet does not provide source section OCR text for page 112, so step wording is grounded primarily in the candidate and attached packet evidence.
The packet does not explicitly state whether production stop is required.
The packet does not explicitly state whether LOTO is required for this installation subsection.
No second-person staffing requirement is explicitly provided for installation in the candidate, even though the related Figure 7-4 artifact retrieval text notes two people for the removal procedure.
